components.name: Hover Card
components.description: Rich preview card that appears on hover or focus — for user profiles, link metadata, and entity details. Content-only panel (trigger is separate). w-72 (288px), bg-card, border-border, rounded-md, p-md, shadow-md. No arrow — use Tooltip for arrow-based hints. Single variant — Side/Align are runtime-only props with no visual difference.

children.name: Bio
children.textContent: The React Framework — created and maintained by @vercel.

examples.name: User Profile
examples.description: Avatar with handle, bio, and join date — the canonical HoverCard content pattern.

props.name: openDelay
props.description: Delay in ms before the card opens after hover starts (HoverCard root)

props.name: closeDelay
props.description: Delay in ms before the card closes after hover ends (HoverCard root)

props.name: asChild
props.description: Merge props onto child element instead of rendering a span (HoverCardTrigger)

props.name: side
props.description: Side of the trigger to render on (HoverCardContent)

props.name: sideOffset
props.description: Distance from the trigger in px (HoverCardContent)

props.name: className
props.description: Additional CSS classes — use w-* to control width (HoverCardContent)

designTokens.description: HoverCardContent padding (p-md) and gap between avatar and info

bestPractices.do: Use HoverCard for supplementary info only — user bio, link preview, metric breakdown. Content must make sense even if the user never sees it.
bestPractices.dont: Put essential content in HoverCard — touch users and keyboard users relying on screen readers may not trigger it.

bestPractices.do: Keep content read-only: text, avatars, badges, and stats. Use openDelay={0} in demos to reduce friction during testing.
bestPractices.dont: Add buttons, forms, or links inside HoverCardContent — use Popover for interactive overlays.

bestPractices.do: Set an explicit width via className (e.g. w-64, w-72) to prevent layout shifts when content length varies.
bestPractices.dont: Let the card width be unconstrained — it will reflow based on content and break alignment.

figmaMapping.codeValue: z-50 w-64 rounded-md border border-border bg-card p-md shadow-md

figmaMapping.value: bottom / top / left / right — no visual diff on card
start / center / end — no visual diff on card

keyboard.action: Focus the trigger to show the hover card

notes: HoverCard opens on both hover and keyboard focus — keyboard users can access content.
Touch devices cannot hover — never put essential content here; use Popover or Dialog instead.
HoverCardContent renders in a portal (z-50) — ensure it does not overlap critical UI.
HoverCardTrigger should always be a focusable element (button or link) — use asChild to avoid nested interactive elements.

related.name: Tooltip
related.desc: Plain text hint on hover. Use instead of HoverCard when content is a single short label with no rich layout.

related.name: Popover
related.desc: Interactive overlay triggered by click. Use when the preview needs buttons, forms, or other interactive elements.

related.name: Dialog
related.desc: Full modal for focused tasks. Use when the entity being previewed needs a dedicated detail view.
